settings.heading: Consulting support for business problems that need structured project thinking
settings.text: Use consulting when the issue is bigger than a single advisory question and needs diagnosis, recommendations, roadmap creation or coordinated improvement across systems, people and operations.

settings.heading: Deeper support before execution
settings.text: Consulting helps shape the problem, define the work and create a practical path before implementation begins.

settings.title: Business systems review
settings.text: Map where tools, workflows, reporting or handovers are creating friction.

settings.title: Process and operating model improvement
settings.text: Clarify ownership, decision rhythm and recurring work before changing how the team operates.

settings.title: Roadmap and scope definition
settings.text: Turn a broad business problem into staged recommendations, priorities and implementation choices.

settings.heading: The problem needs more than advice
settings.text: Consulting sits between advisory and implementation: enough depth to define the work, without pretending every answer is self-serve.

settings.title: You need a practical improvement plan
settings.text: Use consulting to turn a complex issue into a realistic scope, sequence and operating roadmap.

settings.title: Your operating model needs attention
settings.text: Review systems, roles, workflows, reporting and management rhythm before implementation.

settings.title: You may need managed or outsourced support
settings.text: When internal capacity is limited, consulting can define what should be handled externally.

settings.heading: Use consulting when the answer needs a roadmap, not just a recommendation
settings.text: Advisory is useful for a decision point. Consulting is a better fit when the business needs a structured review, multi-step recommendation or project plan before work can be implemented responsibly.

settings.heading: A consulting path with clear checkpoints
settings.text: The process keeps scope, context and next steps visible before larger work begins.

settings.title: Define the issue
settings.text: Start with the business problem, desired outcome and constraints.

settings.title: Review current state
settings.text: Assess workflows, systems, people, reporting or commercial context as needed.

settings.title: Create recommendations
settings.text: Receive practical options, priorities and a realistic path forward.

settings.title: Move into implementation
settings.text: Use the roadmap to brief internal work, request delivery support or choose a service pathway.

settings.heading: Consulting questions
settings.text: Practical answers for choosing consulting instead of advisory, resources or implementation.

settings.question: How do I know consulting is the right fit?
settings.answer: Choose consulting when the issue needs diagnosis, recommendations, scope definition or a roadmap before work can be delivered safely.

settings.question: What happens after purchase or enquiry?
settings.answer: The next step depends on the service. Some products require intake, review, booking or manual delivery before the final output is prepared.

settings.question: Is this suitable for small businesses?
settings.answer: Yes. The consulting pathways are written for SMEs that need practical decisions, workable systems and realistic implementation plans.

settings.heading: Turn the business problem into a workable plan
settings.text: Start with a consulting pathway when the issue needs structure before execution.
